text:
Assistant: The survey confirms: no epic exists, every upstream prerequisite is closed (NMP #2808 authority promotion merged **today** via #2832), and Trellis already has a trace-replay UI (Flight Recorder workstation, trellis#160) that the time-travel phase can reuse. I'll create the epic in `nostr-multi-platform` — that's where 4 of the 5 workstream items and the ADR live, and where the predecessor epic (#2626) lived. Checking its labels first.

Assistant: **Epic created: [nostr-multi-platform#2858](https://github.com/pablof7z/nostr-multi-platform/issues/2858)** — no epic existed before (the surveyor confirmed nothing ties this workstream together; the closest ancestors, NMP #2626/#2627, are closed). I cross-linked it from chirp#30 and NMP#2809, and yes: **time travel is in the plan as committed scope (Phase E), not a stretch goal** — per your message, it's written up as the recorded/replayable debugging capsule with scrubbing, not log viewing. [...] **The plan (five phases in the epic):**

- **Phase A — ADR-0075 amendment** (#2809): define "diagnostic surface" vs "app surface," and update doctrine-lint plus the Trellis public-API leak ratchet (#2642) so the exemption is enforced mechanically, not case-by-case.
- **Phase B — nmp-devtools stream**: ordered open/replace/refresh/close events with owner/provenance/revision/refcount/teardown data; join Trellis `ResourcePlan` → `ApplyDependentInterestDelta` outcomes into bounded retained records (today they're test-only traces); correlate with the existing relay diagnostics; and define the **NMP-owned receipt shape** so Chirp never sees `ResourcePlan`/`ResourceKey`/`Graph`.
- **Phase C — agent-first prover (your CLI/REPL/TUI ask)**: the MCP server (`why_subscription_open`, `why_view_stale`, `what_closed_this_relay`, `list_scope_inventory`, `replay_session`) plus a headless CLI that drives a real Chirp session against real relays and also loads/scrubs exported capsules. Exit criterion baked into the epic: *an agent can answer "why is my feed empty?" from receipts alone.* This lands **before** the pane, so agents can iterate on the diagnostic itself.
- **Phase D — Chirp X-Ray pane** (chirp#30 v1): dev-build-only declared projection/devtools channel; Chirp renders, never parses.
- **Phase E — time travel** (chirp#30 v2): dev-only recorder, ring-buffer retention with explicit export, redaction policy with local/debug vs shareable modes, correlation persisted into the capsule so scrubbing reconstructs full cause chains (refcount 2→1 vs 1→0→CLOSE), and replay-from-capsule-alone so a redacted trace attached to an issue is debuggable without the device or live relays.

One reuse note the surveyor surfaced: Trellis's **Flight Recorder workstation** (trellis#160, merged today) is already a full trace-replay UI — provenance, transaction timeline, receipt inspector, replay tabs. Phase E should feed NMP capsules into it for desktop-side inspection rather than building a second scrubber; the epic says so. [...] 1. Land NMP #2809: amend ADR-0075 so “diagnostic surface” is explicitly allowed while app/native surfaces still cannot expose Trellis types. [...] Assistant: **What the amendment is:** ADR-0075 is the decision that lets NMP use Trellis internally ("private reconciliation substrate") — and its Boundaries section flatly forbids Trellis types in *any* app/native/web-facing API, enforced by doctrine-lint and the leak ratchet. That rule has no carve-out, so `nmp-devtools` — which by definition exposes Trellis trace/audit receipts to tooling — would be a doctrine violation the day it's written. The amendment (#2809) adds a second category to the ADR: a **diagnostic surface** — dev-build-only, never linked by app code, compiled out of release artifacts — where Trellis vocabulary may legally appear. Without it, every phase of the X-Ray epic is fighting the linter; with it, the lint gate knows exactly what's exempt.

**Yes, I can do it** — the writing is mechanical once one genuine architecture question is settled, which the Fable review flagged as a back-edge: the exemption wording depends on *how receipts reach Chirp*. That's your call:

Assistant: **No call needed anymore — the amendment already landed while we were talking.**

**What it is:** ADR-0075 is the ruling that lets NMP use Trellis as its private reconciliation substrate, with a hard boundary: no Trellis vocabulary in any app/native/web-facing surface, enforced by doctrine-lint. The amendment (#2809) adds a second surface category — a **diagnostic surface**: a dev-build-only `nmp-devtools` crate that *may* see Trellis trace/audit data, is never linked by app code, and is compiled out of release builds. Without it, the entire X-Ray epic would be a standing lint violation; with it, the exemption is explicit and machine-checkable. [...] **What it is:** ADR-0075 is the ruling that lets NMP use Trellis as its private reconciliation substrate, with a hard boundary: no Trellis vocabulary in any app/native/web-facing surface, enforced by doctrine-lint. The amendment (#2809) adds a second surface category — a **diagnostic surface**: a dev-build-only `nmp-devtools` crate that *may* see Trellis trace/audit data, is never linked by app code, and is compiled out of release builds. Without it, the entire X-Ray epic would be a standing lint violation; with it, the exemption is explicit and machine-checkable.

**Why you don't have to decide anything:** I was about to draft it myself and asked you the one genuine architecture question (how receipts reach Chirp — sidecar channel vs dev-only FlatBuffer projection). You were away, so I proceeded with the sidecar recommendation — and when I cloned NMP to write the amendment, I found the fabric agents had already landed it and more, all within the last ~90 minutes:

- **PR #2859** — the ADR amendment itself, closing #2809, with the lint classifier scoped to `crates/nmp-devtools/src` and a regression test. **Phase A: done.**
- **PRs #2860/#2861** — `XrayReceipt` + bounded ordered `XrayReceiptStream` + the Trellis→receipt adapter. Most of Phase B.
- **PR #2862** — `XrayCapsule` envelope (schema versions, producer metadata, redaction mode, **symbolication manifest**), stable shareable pseudonymization, and `XrayProbe` helpers for all five debug queries. That's the Fable-review findings implemented — whoever built it worked straight from the updated epic.

And the transport was decided by implementation exactly as I'd have recommended: `nmp-devtools` is a **sidecar with no runtime/app dependents**; receipts never touch the projection pipeline. The only scenario where you'd need to weigh in is if you *disagree* with the sidecar shape — speak now, before Phase D wires Chirp to it.
